sampling_results.note: Subquery HAVING SUM(l_quantity)>300 is extremely selective at SF10: only 624 qualifying keys out of 15M unique l_orderkeys

join_order.description: Iterate all 33554432 slots in lineitem_orderkey_hash index; for each non-empty slot sum l_quantity[positions[offset..offset+count)]; collect keys where sum > 300.0
Scan all 15M orders rows; probe qualifying_orderkeys flat sorted array; collect (o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice) for matches
For each of ~500 qualifying orders, directly index into c_name via c_custkey (TPC-H c_custkey is 1..N sequential, so direct offset: c_name_ptr[c_custkey*26]); no hash table needed
For each of 624 qualifying orderkeys, look up in lineitem_orderkey_hash: hash(ok) -> (offset, count) -> sum l_quantity[positions[offset..offset+count)]; attach sum_qty to matching order group

filter_pushdown.note: Applied as HAVING after group-by aggregation via pre-built index slot scan
Probe flat sorted array of 624 entries during orders sequential scan; binary search or linear scan both O(1) for 624-entry L1-resident array

data_structures.qualifying_orderkeys: flat_sorted_array(624 int32_t entries = 2.5 KB, fits in L1 cache; use std::binary_search for O(log 624) probe, or absl::flat_hash_set<int32_t> pre-reserved(1024))
data_structures.orders_result: struct_of_arrays or vector<OrderRow> of ~500 entries: {int32_t o_orderkey, int32_t o_custkey, int32_t o_orderdate, double o_totalprice, double sum_qty, std::string c_name}
data_structures.customer_access: direct_array: c_name via mmap pointer + c_custkey*26 offset (no hash table, O(1) access, zero build cost)
data_structures.aggregation: flat_vector(~500 groups indexed by position in orders_result; since o_orderkey is PK each qualifying order = exactly 1 group; accumulate sum_qty inline per order)
data_structures.topk: std::partial_sort or std::sort on ~500 elements by (o_totalprice DESC, o_orderdate ASC), take first 100

subquery_index_slot_scan.strategy: parallel OpenMP over 33554432 hash table slots
subquery_index_slot_scan.merge: serial merge of 64 thread-local vectors into global flat sorted array (~624 total keys)

orders_scan.strategy: parallel OpenMP morsel scan of 15M rows
orders_scan.merge: serial concatenation of ~500 total rows across threads

customer_lookup.reason: only ~500 lookups; parallelization overhead exceeds benefit

lineitem_outer_index_lookup.reason: only 624 qualifying keys × ~4 positions = ~2500 accesses; trivial

index_usage.purpose: PHASE 1 (subquery): iterate all 33554432 ht slots; for each non-empty slot (key != INT32_MIN), compute sum(l_quantity) for positions[offset..offset+count); collect slot.key if sum > 300.0 → qualifying_orderkeys. PHASE 2 (outer query sum_qty): for each qualifying orderkey ok, hash(ok) -> probe ht -> (offset,count) -> sum l_quantity[positions[i]] for output sum_qty column.

layout.header: uint32_t num_unique(15000000), uint32_t ht_capacity(33554432), uint32_t num_rows(59986052)
layout.slots: Slot ht[33554432]: {int32_t key, uint32_t offset, uint32_t count} each 12 bytes = 402 MB
layout.positions: uint32_t positions[59986052] = 240 MB, row indices into lineitem columns

memory_layout.lineitem_orderkey_hash_slots: 402 MB, sequential scan across all slots
memory_layout.lineitem_positions_array: 240 MB, accessed sequentially per key group during slot scan
memory_layout.l_quantity_bin: 480 MB, accessed via positions (scattered but grouped per key; with 376 GB RAM all files stay warm in page cache)
memory_layout.orders_columns: o_orderkey(60MB) + o_custkey(60MB) + o_orderdate(60MB) + o_totalprice(120MB) = 300 MB sequential scan
memory_layout.customer_c_name: 39 MB, direct random access by c_custkey offset (500 accesses, negligible)

breakdown.subquery_index_scan: 33554432 slots × 12B = 402 MB + 60M positions × 4B = 240 MB + 60M l_quantity reads × 8B = 480 MB = 1.12 GB; parallel 64 threads at ~50 GB/s cached = ~22 ms
breakdown.customer_lookup: 500 direct array accesses on 39 MB mmap'd file ≈ <1 ms
breakdown.outer_lineitem_index_lookup: 624 hash probes + ~2500 position + l_quantity accesses ≈ <1 ms

estimated_cost.bottleneck: Memory bandwidth for lineitem_orderkey_hash slot scan (1.12 GB); orders scan secondary (300 MB). Both fully parallelized across 64 cores.
